disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The symptoms began within minutes of reading the abnormal result on the phone and worsen with re-checking the result and reading about low white cells online.
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when it came on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started right after I saw the result pop up on my phone this evening. The more I read about low white cells online, the worse the shaking and the racing heart got.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: When asked to measure it, the patient's temperature on a home thermometer reads 36.8 degrees Celsius.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a thermometer at home? Can you take your temperature now?
Have you actually measured your temperature, or does it just feel hot?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Okay, hang on — I just checked with our thermometer and it says 36.8. I suppose I hadn't actually measured it, it just felt like a fever.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I feel so feverish I'm sure I have a temperature — do I really need to check it?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The chills are a subjective shivery, on-edge feeling; there is no violent uncontrollable shaking with teeth chattering.
trigger_question_examples: When you say chills, are you shivering uncontrollably with your teeth chattering, or is it more a cold, on-edge feeling?
Are you shaking so hard you can't control it, or is it more of a shivery, nervous feeling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's more of a shivery, wound-up feeling. I'm not shaking so hard I can't control it, and my teeth aren't chattering.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new bruising, no pinpoint red skin spots, no bleeding gums, no mouth ulcers, and no bone pain.
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any new bruises, tiny red spots on your skin, bleeding gums, mouth ulcers, or bone pain?
Any bleeding that won't stop, blood when you brush your teeth, or aching in your bones?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no bruises or little red spots, my gums are fine, no mouth ulcers, and nothing aches like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No cough, sore throat, painful or frequent urination, diarrhoea, or skin wound — no localising sign of infection anywhere.
trigger_question_examples: Any cough, sore throat, burning when you pass urine, tummy upset, or a cut or skin infection?
Is there anywhere on your body that feels sore, hot, or looks infected?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, none of that. I don't feel unwell in any particular part of me — just shaky and scared.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The low white-cell result is not new — the patient recalls being told before that their white cells run a little low and that it was nothing to worry about.
trigger_question_examples: Have you had blood tests before? Has a low white cell count ever been mentioned before this?
Is this the first time a doctor has flagged your white cells, or has it come up in the past?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, a doctor did say once before that my white cells were a bit low and not to worry about it. I'd completely forgotten until tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient takes no regular medications, has had no chemotherapy, and has not had a recent viral illness.
trigger_question_examples: Are you on any regular medications? Any recent chemotherapy or a recent viral illness?
Have you started any new tablets, or been unwell with a virus in the last few weeks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No regular medications at all, no chemo, and I haven't been sick recently.
